description:
A technical writer wants to tidy up a single–file outline that is currently sitting loose in their home directory.  
Your goal is to:

1. Create the following directory structure (create any intermediate directories that are missing):  
   • /home/user/documentation/source  
   • /home/user/documentation/review  

2. Move the file named architecture_outline.md that is presently in /home/user into the new source directory so that its new, canonical location is:  
   /home/user/documentation/source/architecture_outline.md  

3. Inside /home/user/documentation/review create a **relative** symbolic link named architecture_outline.md that points **up** one level and then into source, i.e. it must resolve to:  
   ../source/architecture_outline.md  

4. Produce a one-line log file at /home/user/symlink_report.log that records the link you just created.  
   The log file must have exactly this format (no leading “./” prefixes, no extra spaces, tabs, or trailing slash):  

   architecture_outline.md -> ../source/architecture_outline.md  

Automated checks will confirm:

• The directory tree exists exactly as specified.  
• The original architecture_outline.md no longer sits directly in /home/user.  
• /home/user/documentation/review/architecture_outline.md is a symbolic link whose target is the relative path ../source/architecture_outline.md.  
• /home/user/symlink_report.log contains exactly one line, identical to the line shown above, followed by a single trailing newline.  

Complete the task in the terminal; do not echo any additional text to the log file. 2-3 well-chosen commands are enough, but use as many as you need.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):
----------------------------------------
1. File: /home/user/architecture_outline.md  
   Contents (3 lines, final newline at end of file):
   # System Architecture
   Initial outline.

2. No directory named /home/user/documentation currently exists.

Expected end state (after the agent finishes):
----------------------------------------------
1. Directory /home/user/documentation/source exists.  
2. File /home/user/documentation/source/architecture_outline.md exists with **exactly** the same two lines (and trailing newline) as the original file shown above.  
3. Directory /home/user/documentation/review exists.  
4. /home/user/documentation/review/architecture_outline.md is a symbolic link whose link target (as shown by `readlink -n`) is ../source/architecture_outline.md  
   • The link is relative, not absolute.  
5. The original file /home/user/architecture_outline.md no longer exists (it was moved).  
6. Log file /home/user/symlink_report.log exists, is a regular text file, world-readable, and its entire contents are precisely (including the trailing newline):

architecture_outline.md -> ../source/architecture_outline.md

(no other characters before or after).

Any deviation from these exact paths, filenames, link targets, or log-file contents means the task has not been completed correctly.
name: task_000000_9eb1a48c
